docs.title: CubeBall Documentation
docs.text: Football gameplay for Bukkit ecosystems with a configurable falling-block ball, arena markers, match flow automation, and admin tooling. This documentation covers installation, arena setup, commands, and gameplay tuning.

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text: Get Started Arena Setup Configuration

docs.title: What CubeBall Provides
docs.text:
• A physical ball represented by a configurable falling block
  • Match creation directly in game from arena marker blocks
  • Team management for blue, red, and spectator roles
  • Automatic countdowns, round restarts, score handling, and overtime
  • Configurable particles, sounds, title timings, bounce tuning, and goal animations

docs.title: Community
docs.text:
• GitHub repository
  • CurseForge page
  • Bukkit page
  • Discord support

docs.title: Arena Setup
docs.text: CubeBall detects an arena by scanning blocks around the admin running /cb match.

docs.title: Marker Blocks
docs.text:
Default markers from config.yml:

  • Ball spawn: EMERALD_BLOCK
  • Blue team spawn: BLUE_WOOL
  • Red team spawn: RED_WOOL
  • Blue goal: BLUE_CONCRETE
  • Red goal: RED_CONCRETE

docs.title: Placement Rules
docs.text:
• Place one ball spawn marker where the center kickoff point should be.
  • Place one or more spawn markers for each team.
  • Build each goal line using the corresponding goal marker material.
  • Stand close enough to the arena before running /cb match, within match.scan-radius.

docs.title: How Positions Are Derived
docs.text:
• Ball spawn is created 3 blocks above the ball marker and centered on the block.
  • Team spawns are created 2 blocks above each team marker.
  • Goal detection uses the X/Z position of the goal marker blocks.

docs.title: Typical Setup Flow
docs.text:
1. Build the field.
  2. Place the marker blocks.
  3. Run /cb match.
  4. Confirm all sections report OK.
  5. Assign teams with /cb team.
  6. Start the match with /cb start.

docs.title: Commands And Permissions
docs.text: The main command is /cb.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
/cb reload : Reloads config.yml. Available from console and to players with cubeball.manage.

/cb match : Scans the nearby arena markers and prepares a match.

/cb start : Starts the configured match and launches the countdown.

/cb stop : Cancels the current match.

/cb pause : Pauses the current match and removes the active ball.

/cb resume : Restarts a paused match with a new countdown.

/cb team <BLUE|RED|SPECTATOR> <player> : Assigns a player to a team.

/cb generate <id> : Spawns a ball at the executing player's location.

/cb generate <id> <x> <y> <z> <world> : Spawns a ball at an explicit position.

/cb remove <id> : Removes a tracked ball by identifier.

docs.title: Permission
docs.text: cubeball.manage : Grants access to management commands. Defaults to server operators.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Console support is intentionally limited to reload, generate, and remove.
  • Tab completion is available for admins and suggests commands, teams, tracked ball IDs, and worlds.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: CubeBall stores its settings in plugins/CubeBall/config.yml. After editing the file, apply changes with /cb reload.

docs.title: Ball
docs.text:
ball.material : Falling block material used for the ball.

ball.drop-item : Allows the ball to drop as an item if the entity breaks.

ball.invulnerable : Protects the ball entity on server versions that support invulnerability.

ball.spawn-particle.* : Controls particle type, amount, offsets, and speed when a ball is spawned.

docs.title: Match
docs.text:
match.duration-seconds : Total match duration in seconds.

match.max-goals : Ends the match once one team reaches this amount. 0 means unlimited.

match.scan-radius : Radius used by /cb match to find arena marker blocks.

match.countdown-step-ticks : Delay between 3, 2, 1, and GO.

match.round-restart-delay-ticks : Delay before restarting after a goal.

match.title.* : Controls title fade timings and the sound played for match messages.

match.goal-animation.firework.enabled : Spawns fireworks on goal celebration.

match.goal-animation.effect.* : Controls the world effect played on goal blocks.

docs.title: Arena Materials
docs.text:
arena.materials.ball-spawn : Marker block used to place the match ball spawn.

arena.materials.blue-team-spawn : Marker block used for blue player spawn points.

arena.materials.red-team-spawn : Marker block used for red player spawn points.

arena.materials.blue-team-goal : Marker block used for the blue goal line.

arena.materials.red-team-goal : Marker block used for the red goal line.

docs.title: Physics
docs.text:
physics.player-search-radius : Distance used to find nearby players around the ball.

physics.player-direct-hit-distance : Collision distance for a direct kick.

physics.player-column-hit-distance : Extra collision distance when player and ball share the same X/Z block column.

physics.kick.y-velocity.* : Vertical boost applied when standing, sneaking, or sprinting.

physics.block-bounce.* : Bounce detection thresholds used by the repeating update task.

physics.listener-bounce.* : Settings used when the server attempts to place the falling block.

physics.ball-hit-sound.* : Sound name, volume, pitch, and minimum impact threshold for ball impacts.

docs.title: Tasks
docs.text:
tasks.match-timer-period-ticks : Period of the match countdown scheduler.

tasks.ball-update-period-ticks : Period of the main ball physics scheduler.

docs.title: Local Development
docs.text:
Build the plugin:

./gradlew test shadowJar

Build the documentation locally:

pip install -r requirements.txt
mkdocs serve

docs.title: Repository Conventions
docs.text:
• Java 17 is required
  • The plugin artifact is produced with the Shadow plugin
  • Documentation is published through GitHub Actions and Stellionix/docs-common

docs.title: Pull Requests
docs.text:
• Keep gameplay behavior covered by tests when possible
  • Update docs/ when commands, config, or installation steps change
  • Ensure ./gradlew test shadowJar still passes before submitting

docs.title: Download
docs.text: CubeBall is distributed as a Bukkit plugin JAR.

docs.title: Official Pages
docs.text:
• CurseForge
  • Bukkit Dev
  • GitHub Releases

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Minecraft Java Edition server
  • Bukkit-compatible software such as Paper or Spigot
  • Java 17 for current builds

docs.title: Build From Source
docs.text:
./gradlew shadowJar

The built plugin JAR is generated under build/libs/.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text: CubeBall turns a configurable falling block into a football-like entity. Players move into the ball to kick it, and the plugin keeps the entity alive by recreating it when the server tries to place the block.

docs.title: Match Lifecycle
docs.text:
1. An admin marks the arena with specific blocks for the ball spawn, team spawns, and goals.
  2. /cb match scans the nearby area and validates those markers.
  3. Players are assigned to blue, red, or spectator teams.
  4. /cb start launches a countdown, teleports teams, and spawns the match ball.
  5. Goals update the score, trigger effects, and restart the round.
  6. The match ends on time limit or max goals, with overtime if scores are tied.

docs.title: Ball Behavior
docs.text:
• The ball is a falling block using the ball.material setting.
  • Player movement adds velocity to the ball.
  • Sneaking and sprinting use different vertical kick boosts.
  • Bounce handling is split between the scheduled physics task and the block-change listener.
  • Optional particles and sounds are played when the ball spawns or impacts.

docs.title: Arena Discovery
docs.text:
Arena setup is block-driven. CubeBall scans a radius around the admin and detects:

  • One ball spawn marker
  • Blue team spawn markers
  • Red team spawn markers
  • Blue goal markers
  • Red goal markers

See Arena Setup for the exact marker list.

docs.title: Install The Plugin
docs.text:
1. Download the latest CubeBall JAR.
  2. Stop the Minecraft server.
  3. Put the JAR into the server plugins/ directory.
  4. Start the server.
  5. Wait for CubeBall to generate its default config.yml.

docs.title: First Startup Check
docs.text:
Expected log output:

[CubeBall] Enabling CubeBall vX.Y.Z

If the plugin loads correctly, you can reload its settings later with:

/cb reload

docs.title: Recommended Server Software
docs.text:
• Paper for the best runtime behavior
  • Spigot for broad compatibility
  • Other Bukkit-compatible forks may work if they preserve falling block behavior

docs.title: Next Step
docs.text: Continue with Arena Setup to create a playable field.

docs.title: /cb match Reports KO
docs.text:
Possible causes:

  • No ball spawn marker was found
  • One team has no spawn markers
  • One goal side has no goal markers
  • The arena is outside match.scan-radius
  • Marker materials in the world do not match config.yml

docs.title: The Ball Falls Incorrectly Or Stops Too Often
docs.text:
Check these settings:

  • physics.block-bounce.threshold
  • physics.listener-bounce.divisor
  • physics.listener-bounce.max-y-velocity
  • physics.listener-bounce.stop-velocity-threshold
  • physics.listener-bounce.stop-min-y-velocity

Paper is recommended if entity behavior differs on your server fork.

docs.title: Players Cannot Use Admin Commands
docs.text:
Verify one of these is true:

  • The player is operator
  • The player has the cubeball.manage permission

docs.title: Configuration Changes Do Not Apply
docs.text:
Run:

/cb reload

If the issue persists, check startup logs for invalid enum values in materials, particles, sounds, or effects.
